question:
Why is publicly praising one employee's work in a way that implicitly demeans others generally considered a poor management practice?
A) Praise is legally restricted to private settings in most labor agreements.
B) It motivates the praised employee so intensely they become unmanageable.
C) It creates a competitive, zero-sum atmosphere that undermines team cohesion and can humiliate those not mentioned.
D) Other employees will immediately quit upon hearing a colleague complimented.

Compute the resul tand return the index of the correct answer from the provided list. You MUST end your response with exactly one final line in this format: Answer: <LETTER>.

C) It creates a competitive, zero-sum atmosphere that undermines team cohesion and can humiliate those not mentioned.
D) Other employees will immediately quit upon hearing a colleague complimented.
